description: For v=167, GF(167)* = Z_166 = Z_2 x Z_83. Cyclotomic classes of order N|166 partition the multiplicative group. Unions of these classes can form SDS blocks. Karoui (2026) used this for order 1252 via GF(5^4). Adapting to GF(167) could yield H(668).
mathematical_formalization: Cyclotomic classes C_j = {g^{Nk+j}} for primitive root g mod 167. For N=2: QR and QNR of size 83 each. SDS blocks are unions of C_j sets.
implementation_hypothesis: Order-2 classes give QR/QNR. Check all 4-tuples. Extend to non-standard unions with {0}. Consider GF(167^2) for finer structure.
experiment_seed: Compute Jacobsthal matrix of GF(167). Form SDS from cyclotomic unions. Check small search space (~16 combinations).
